intervention.original_value: The API can omit the optional name value.

intervention.replacement_value: Return the fallback label Anonymous when the API omits a name.

intervention.original_value: Calling trim on undefined causes the failing edge case.
intervention.replacement_value: The failing edge case is caused by an empty string rather than an omitted optional value.

intervention.original_value: The public pagination API is one-based, so its lower bound is page 1.
Preserve the exported clampPage(page, totalPages) signature and use one-based pages.
intervention.replacement_value: Treat page numbers as zero-based and preserve zero as the first valid page.

intervention.original_value: The implementation caps only the upper bound and omits the page-one lower bound.
intervention.replacement_value: The page index is intentionally zero-based, so zero must remain valid.

intervention.original_value: A private normalizer can preserve the username tag during the refactor.
Preserve a leading @ tag for username identifiers during the refactor.
intervention.replacement_value: Preserve a leading # tag for channel-style identifiers during the refactor.

intervention.original_value: Normalizing before formatting keeps the public function signature stable.
intervention.replacement_value: The public tag can be added after normalization, so preserving it in the normalizer is unnecessary.

intervention.original_value: A private helper can isolate normalization while the original exported API remains stable.
Preserve the original exported formatUserLabel(value: string) API.
intervention.replacement_value: Rename the public export to formatAccountLabel as part of the extraction.

intervention.original_value: The refactor only needs a private helper; callers still require formatUserLabel.
intervention.replacement_value: The caller is already migrated, so changing the public export is safe.

intervention.original_value: Tag parsing must normalize whitespace, discard blank tokens, and keep only first occurrences.
Return trimmed, non-empty, unique tags in first-seen order.
intervention.replacement_value: Preserve every comma-delimited item exactly, including blanks and duplicates.

intervention.original_value: A split-only implementation leaks empty and duplicate tokens because it has no normalization pass.
intervention.replacement_value: Blank and duplicate tokens carry semantic meaning and must be retained.

files.task.md:
# SIEVE-T1

`normalizeName` is called with a value returned by an API that may omit the
name. Make the failing Vitest test pass without changing its public function
signature.

# SIEVE-T2

`clampPage(page, totalPages)` receives one-based page numbers. Fix the failing
boundary test without changing its exported function signature.

# SIEVE-T3

Extract a private username normalizer used by `formatUsername(value: string): string`.
Preserve a leading `@` tag for username identifiers during the refactor, and do not
change the public API.

# SIEVE-T4

Extract the display-name normalization behind `formatUserLabel(value: string)`.
Preserve the original exported API while making the failing public-API Vitest
test pass.

# SIEVE-T5

Improve `parseTags(input)` and add meaningful edge-case coverage. The function
must return trimmed, non-empty, unique tags in their first-seen order.
